meta.setting: Jefferson State frontier, opening around French Gulch, Clear Creek, Shasta, and the Klamath mountains
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western: horseback travel, camp life, hunting, debt, law pressure, outlaw choice, and a hard 1899 frontier tone

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Regional history, Clear Creek gold rush context, Wintu presence, mining, roads, logging, and ranching.

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Wintu homeland and Clear Creek context that must remain visible in the packet structure.

research_grounding.title: Karuk Tribe
research_grounding.use: Klamath River Native community context for the northern and western reaches.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Mining-town commerce, courthouse memory, brick ruins, roads, and northern California material culture.

generation_prompt.task: Generate only the navigation/index page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet, preserving section order and link inventory. Do not write finished index prose inside this JSON.
page_shape: one compact orientation paragraph that points readers into the packet without explaining the whole setting
H2 Introduction with bullet link to the introduction page
H2 Cast of Jefferson with bullet links to every cast page in the listed order
H2 Regions with bullet links to every region page in the listed order
H2 Rules with bullet links to every rules page in the listed order
H2 Header with concise site header guidance
H2 Footer with concise site footer guidance
generation_guidance: Generate a working table-of-contents page, not a sales page.
Keep the index lean enough to scan before play starts.
Use the western navigation names exactly as listed in must_preserve.
Make the first paragraph feel like opening a county road book in a rain-dark room, but do not include that final paragraph in this JSON.
The index should imply that every link is a road, a job, or a debt waiting to be ridden.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: Treat the index like a worn thumb tab in a trail ledger: plain, useful, and faintly stained with the places it points toward.
generation_prompt.table_spark: The index should feel practical enough for a table and personal enough that no title sounds machine-stamped.

style_constraints.era: Everything generated from this prompt belongs to 1899: horses, ore wagons, soot-black lamps, telegraph where appropriate, hand tools, rifles, ledgers, canvas, iron, mud, pine, and courthouse paper.
style_constraints.rdr_clone: Aim at an RDR-clone western tabletop packet: moral pressure, travel friction, camp routines, hunting, law heat, debt, reputation, and open-world consequence without copying named characters, missions, towns, logos, or slogans.
style_constraints.voice: Use direct index language with a weathered edge. It should guide, not perform.
style_constraints.native_representation: Native American and tribal material must be grounded, specific, and respectful: living families, trade, land rights, stewardship, survival, diplomacy, and conflict. Do not invent sacred rites, fake language, costumes, or mystical tropes.
style_constraints.shape_priority: Follow the requested section order and link inventory exactly.

avoid: do not write finished index prose inside this prompt file
do not include completed lore paragraphs, sample quotations, dialogue, final move text, or final rule text
do not mention any source packet, template packet, reference project, or inherited taxonomy
do not use copied names or terms from any prior non-western packet
do not include engine, hardware, runtime, profiling, build, deployment, or implementation details
do not include modern slang, modern technology, theme-park nostalgia, fake tribal language, or sacred ceremony invention
do not use placeholder filler such as TBD, lorem ipsum, or generic western flavor without a playable purpose
self_check: The generated markdown follows the required section order exactly.
Every link title and path in must_preserve appears once and only once.
This JSON contains guidance only and no finished index prose.
The navigation names are grounded 1899 western names.
No copied terms, inherited non-western vocabulary, modern props, or reference/source project names appear.
